source_boundary.notes: Rows are arXiv metadata receipts and requirements pressure only. They are not treated as proof of external quantum hardware, surface-code, decoder, resource-estimation, cryptographic, or quantum-advantage claims.

workbench_requirements: Every quantum error-correction claim declares code, supported error model, stabilizers, logical operators, syndrome bits, decoder table or model, correction action, and negative controls.
Surface-code, LDPC, neural-decoder, and hardware claims require primary code/benchmark cards, noise model, code distance, threshold or logical-error-rate evidence, and independent verifier receipts.
Fault-tolerant compiler claims require circuit IR, gate set, resource estimate, rewrite/proof log, and replayed equivalence or bounded simulation receipts.
Quantum advantage and resource-estimation claims remain blocked unless task definition, classical baseline, cost model, hardware assumptions, and reproducibility evidence are present.
BuildLang/buildc should carry typed Pauli operators, stabilizer groups, syndrome tables, circuit IR, decoder outputs, and resource-estimation receipts after the JavaScript fixture stabilizes.
